2606.08792.title: The Amplifying Mirror: Locating and Steering the Partisan Direction inside a Large Language Model
2606.08792.paper_summary: Large language models are rapicly replacing search engines as the primary interface between people and information. Unlike search engines, which retrieve existing content, LLMs generate novel text shaped by internal representations learned during training. Here we show that partisan political identity is encoded in the model's activation space, and that this direction directly shapes generation. Using 190,491 tweets from sitting members of the U.S. Congress as labeled training data, we train linear probes on the hidden states of the Llama 3.1 8B Instruct model. We identify a single geometric axis at layer 18 that separates Republican from Democratic text with an AUC of 0.945 and a Cohen's d of 1.94, and use sparse autoencoders to decompose that axis into interpretable partisan features. Causally intervening along this axis, ablating or amplifying the partisan component mid-generation, produces systematic shifts in the model's output. We witness stance reversals, register shifting, and structured fabrications of authority. Our results demonstrate that partisan bias in language models is not a vague emergent property but a learned geometric feature that can be precisely located and steered. Partisan bias is not a bug to be patched, but a structural property of how these models encode information about their users. As LLMs displace search engines as the interface to knowledge, understanding that product design (and its consequences) will be essential for navigating the legal, social, and political transitions from an information ecosystem that is curated to one that is generated.
2606.08792.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型正在迅速取代搜尋引擎，成為人們與資訊之間的主要介面。與檢索現有內容的搜尋引擎不同，LLMs 生成由訓練過程中學習到的內部表徵塑造的新文本。在這裡，我們展示了黨派政治身份是如何編碼在模型的激活空間中，並且這一方向直接影響生成。使用來自美國國會現任成員的 190,491 條推文作為標記訓練數據，我們在 Llama 3.1 8B Instruct 模型的隱藏狀態上訓練線性探針。我們在第 18 層識別出一個單一的幾何軸，該軸以 0.945 的 AUC 和 1.94 的 Cohen's d 將共和黨文本與民主黨文本分開，並使用稀疏自編碼器將該軸分解為可解釋的黨派特徵。在這一軸上進行因果干預，削弱或增強黨派成分，在生成過程中產生系統性的輸出變化。我們目睹了立場的反轉、登記的轉變以及權威的結構性虛構。我們的結果表明，語言模型中的黨派偏見不是一種模糊的自發性特徵，而是一種可以精確定位和引導的學習幾何特徵。黨派偏見不是需要修補的錯誤，而是這些模型如何編碼用戶資訊的結構性特徵。隨著 LLMs 取代搜尋引擎作為知識的介面，理解產品設計（及其後果）將對於從一個經過策展的信息生態系統過渡到一個生成的信息生態系統至關重要。
2606.08792.author: Wendy K. Tam et.al.
